mentions.note: Buffett continues to like stock despite selling some; his approval from Becky Quick interview contradicts sell-side analysts and makes them look silly.
Jim (Florida caller) held since 2013 spinoff; stock up quite a bit. Cramer confirms doing really well, recommends holding and buying any dips.
Up 4% today is good opportunity to sell; down 35% YTD. No CEO or CFO; eviscerated by Figma and Canva. $89B market cap unsustainable.
Bill Mus caller question. Stock down hard with hyperscalers; Cramer likes it but recommends small start due to losses and volatility.
Called 'dead money'—spending portions on AI with no visible return yet; borrowing without clear data center ROI path forward.
Carl (83-year-old caller, down 30% on sourcing disputes) asks about it. Cramer recommends buy—non-combustible power for data centers; Brookfield loves it.
Passed $15 trillion AUM hurdle with superb quarter; sense of multi-day move coming as it pulls away from competition.
Up 290% since Vince took over Aug 2022; outperforming all big banks and trust bank peers. Multi-day move expected; Cramer pounded table.
Down nearly 10% despite best numbers and potential market share gains from IBM; component stock collapse suggests market stress despite fundamentals.
Tom (Texas caller) holds good position. Cramer doubles down—6% yield, want to own it for fixed income, terrific play.
CEO Jordan reports solid Q, loan growth strong, credit quality excellent, regional economy booming across Sunbelt. Every dip has been buying opportunity.
Buffett's surprise purchase signals major tech endorsement; stock jumped 3% on news. Start of potential wonderful relationship like Apple.
Cramer thought Goldman fabulous; financials show strength though one-day pop nature limits staying power.
Fred (California caller) holding since 2013; world's largest single-crystal turbine blade producer for jet engines and data center turbines. Remarkable company; every dip is a buy.
Adam (Florida caller) asked about blockchain disruption; Cramer says doesn't make him want to get away from ICE—owns everybody, inexpensive here. BNY better buy.
Carpet-bombed despite beat and raise; cardio franchise couple-hundred-million miss on $100B business triggered outsized selloff—not justified by results.
Brett (Maryland caller) asks about building materials outlook. Cramer passes—too hard to own in rising-rate uncertainty despite company quality.
Called 'rotten fruit'—still coasting on merchant-of-computing narrative from week ago; unclear if rally justified by fundamentals.
City analyst report contradicts realist concerns about Copilot and Azure; stock jumped 3%. Cramer taking abstract view over fears of anthropic client switching.
Tumbled 8% despite strong data center memory demand fundamentals; frighteningly negative performance conflicts with best numbers in portfolio.
Jeff (South Africa living caller) bought on dip, stock keeps dipping 10% past 3 months but numbers solid. Cramer recommends buy—road builder should be bought, likely consolidation candidate.
Dennis (North Carolina caller) notes CEO was on show, story loved, stock cut in half. Cramer says pure spec—nuclear too darn expensive, not happening.
